name: Santander-Mediterranean Greenway: Abejar - Hontoria del Pinar Loop
name_original: Hontoria del Pinar desde Abejar — circular por la Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo
description: This difficult 44.5-mile touring cycling route from Abejar explores the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway with varied terrain and historic si
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Abejar traverse a landscape characterized by extensive Urbión pine forests and the initial basin of the Duero River. The region offers varied terrain, from the gentle slopes near the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir to more challenging ascents providing views of the Urbión and Cebollera mountains. Cyclists can explore natural features such as the clear waters of La Fuentona de Muriel and historical elements like the submerged Roman bridge at low water levels. Best touring cycling routes around Abejar The most popular touring cycling route is Hontoria del Pinar from Abejar — circular via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, a 44.5 miles (71.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows a historic greenway through varied scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir – Molinos de Duero loop from Abejar, a moderate 17.6 miles (28.4 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and passes through the village of Molinos de Duero. Local touring cyclists also love the Abejar – Ermita de La Blanca loop from Abejar, a 33.4 miles (53.8 km) trail leading through the local countryside towards the Ermita de La Blanca, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Abejar is defined by pine forests, the Duero River basin, and views of the Urbión and Cebollera mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult long-distance routes. The routes in Abejar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 160 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Abejar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Abejar?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Abejar, offering a diverse range of options for exploring the region's landscapes.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Abejar?
A: The routes around Abejar cater to various skill levels. You'll find 2 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 14 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual riders and those seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available from Abejar?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Abejar are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40312273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir – Molinos de Duero loop from Abejar&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036846563/hontoria-del-pinar-from-abejar-circular-via-the-santander-mediterranean-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hontoria del Pinar from Abejar — circular via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt; also offers a circular experience.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while cycling around Abejar?
A: Cycling around Abejar offers views of the extensive Urbión pine forests and the initial basin of the Duero River. You can also visit natural attractions like the exceptionally clear waters of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4709888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fuentona de Muriel&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. From higher points, you'll enjoy expansive vistas of the Urbión and Cebollera mountains.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest accessible by touring bike near Abejar?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2620662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de La Blanca&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century hermitage, or explore routes that may offer glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Submerged Roman Bridge of Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt; when water levels are low at the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir. The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt; itself is also a notable point of interest.

Q: Are there any greenways or dedicated cycling paths in the Abejar area?
A: Yes, the area features greenways that are excellent for touring cycling. A notable example is the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036846563/hontoria-del-pinar-from-abejar-circular-via-the-santander-mediterranean-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hontoria del Pinar from Abejar — circular via the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt; route, offering a unique cycling experience on a historic path.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Abejar?
A: The region around Abejar is generally best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the pine forests, but it's advisable to check local weather forecasts for optimal conditions.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Abejar?
A: Abejar, as a gateway to the Urbión pine forests, typically offers various parking options within the town and at trailheads, making it convenient for cyclists to start their routes. Specific parking availability may vary by starting point, so it's good to check local signage.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Abejar?
A: The touring cycling routes in Abejar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 14 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the Urbión pine forests, and the scenic views of the Duero River basin and surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40312273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir – Molinos de Duero loop from Abejar&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to offer beautiful views of the reservoir and passes through the charming village of Molinos de Duero.

Q: Can I find routes that lead to specific shelters or points of interest for a break?
A: Certainly. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinos de Duero – MOJABRAGAS Shelter loop from Abejar&lt;/a&gt; are designed to include specific points of interest or shelters, providing convenient spots for rest and enjoying the surroundings during your ride.

Q: Are there any touring routes that explore the countryside towards local hermitages?
A: Yes, you can explore the local countryside and reach historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31491405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abejar – Ermita de La Blanca loop from Abejar&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading you through scenic landscapes towards the notable Ermita de La Blanca.
tips: This part of the greenway is less crowded so it is somewhat invaded by vegetation
There is a generous fountain at the entrance

It is known as "La Puerta de Pinares" since it opens the way to the Urbión pine forests and the initial basin of the Duero. Among its monuments are:
-Parish Church of San Juan Bautista: Gothic, from the 16th-17th centuries.
-Ntra. Sra. del Camino hermitage, from the 18th century.
-Ruins of a Moorish tower or watchtower: It preserves about 3 meters of its original height. The wall is 1 meter thick, and its interior has a diameter of 3.5 meters. The interior is accessed through the base. It is built in masonry.
-Watchtower viewpoint, from where you can see the Urbión and Cebollera mountains.
-Museum of Cycling.
We have fountains, several accommodations and a restaurant (I recommend the one at the Repsol Gas Station, always available and with exquisite Tortilla skewers)

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness. While sections on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway are generally flat, the full 71.6 km loop includes more demanding parts with an elevation gain of nearly 500 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of bicycle is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is generally suitable for touring bicycles, even those with 28mm tires, especially on the compacted gravel surface of the Greenway. However, a gravel bike might offer a more comfortable ride on some looser sections or more challenging parts of the circular route.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Abejar. Parking is typically available within the village of Abejar, which serves as a convenient starting and ending point for the loop.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: On average, this 71.6 km circular route takes about 4 hours and 39 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway section?
faqs.answer: The section of the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway used in this route is a converted railway line, featuring a relatively flat, compacted gravel surface. This makes for a smooth ride on most parts, though some sections might be looser.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse scenery including alternating cereal crop fields, serene oak groves, and dense pine forests. The route also passes through charming villages with traditional architecture and repurposed former railway stations that offer rest stops.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the Chezeaux Abandoned Train Station, the Ermita de La Blanca, and the Pinar Grande Abandoned Building. The starting point, Abejar, is also a notable settlement.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, 71.6 km distance, and nearly 500 meters of elevation gain, this specific circular route is not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires very good fitness and some cycling experience.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Santander-Mediterranean Greenway itself is generally accessible, the suitability for dogs on a 71.6 km touring bicycle route depends on your dog's endurance and whether they can comfortably travel alongside or in a trailer for such a long distance. Always ensure dogs are leashed and follow local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route, as it primarily uses public roads and the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially in the midday sun, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially adverse weather.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drink, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, you will pass through villages like Abejar where you can find services. Some former railway stations along the greenway have been repurposed and may offer amenities like hostels, rest areas, and information panels.
